title: Children's Ibuprofen Dosage by Weight 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Ibuprofen and Acetaminophen Dosing for Children by Weight

fields.label: Child's weight (kg)
fields.help: Weight in kilograms. Weigh your child without clothes.

explanation:
**Pediatric dosing guidelines** are based on clinical studies and recommendations from organizations like the WHO and CDC. Dosing varies by age, weight, medication type, and individual factors. These are reference values; your child's medical history always takes priority.

## How dosing is calculated

Pediatric medication dosing by weight uses the formula: **dose (mg) = mg/kg × body weight (kg)**. This calculator applies the formula based on your inputs and alerts you to any doses outside typical safe ranges.

## Standard pediatric dosing reference

| Medication | Dose Range | Interval | Maximum Daily |
|---|---|---|---|
| Ibuprofen | 5–10 mg/kg | Every 6–8 hours | 40 mg/kg/day |
| Acetaminophen | 10–15 mg/kg | Every 4–6 hours | 75 mg/kg/day |

## Important notes

This calculation is an educational reference. For medical decisions, always consult your pediatrician. Dosages are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly. Never mix medications without pediatrician approval.

faq.q: What is the correct ibuprofen dose for a child?
faq.a: Ibuprofen: 5–10 mg/kg every 6–8 hours. Maximum 40 mg/kg per day. Always follow your pediatrician's recommendations for your child's specific age and condition.

faq.q: How much acetaminophen can I give my child?
faq.a: Acetaminophen: 10–15 mg/kg every 4–6 hours. Maximum 75 mg/kg per day. Do not exceed recommended intervals to prevent overdose.

faq.q: Can I give ibuprofen and acetaminophen together?
faq.a: Not without pediatrician approval. Mixing medications increases overdose risk. Consult your doctor before combining fever-reducing treatments.

faq.q: At what age can a child take ibuprofen?
faq.a: Ibuprofen is approved for children 6 months and older. Acetaminophen is safe from birth. Always verify dosing with your pediatrician first.

faq.q: How often can I give my child fever medication?
faq.a: Ibuprofen every 6–8 hours; acetaminophen every 4–6 hours. Do not exceed maximum daily doses. Keep a log to avoid accidental overdosing.

faq.q: How do I calculate medication dose by weight?
faq.a: Use the formula: dose (mg) = mg/kg × child's weight (kg). For example, 10 mg/kg × 15 kg = 150 mg per dose.

faq.q: What liquid medicine concentration should I use?
faq.a: Common concentrations: ibuprofen 40 mg/mL (200 mg/5mL), acetaminophen 24 mg/mL (120 mg/5mL). Always check the bottle label before measuring.

faq.q: When should I call my child's doctor about fever?
faq.a: Contact your pediatrician if fever lasts more than 72 hours, if your child seems very ill, or if medication doesn't help. Seek emergency care for severe symptoms.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
